questions.question: Which flag does GitHub Pages pass to `jekyll build` on its servers, and what is its effect?
choices: `--safe`, which loads only safelisted plugins and ignores `_plugins/`
`--incremental`, which caches partial builds for speed
questions.explanation: GitHub Pages runs `jekyll build --safe`. That mode refuses to load gems outside the published safelist and ignores arbitrary Ruby in `_plugins/`. There is no error - non-safelisted gems are silently dropped.

questions.question: You added `jekyll-picture-tag` in Module 4 for responsive images. Locally, `bundle exec jekyll build` produces `` elements. After deploying via the built-in GitHub Pages build, your live posts have plain `` tags and no error appears in the Actions log. What is the most likely cause?
choices: GitHub Pages stripped the `<picture>` tags as part of its HTML sanitiser
The CDN is caching an older build; purge it and the tags will appear
`jekyll-picture-tag` is not on the GitHub Pages plugin safelist, so the production `--safe` build refuses to load it
The `Gemfile.lock` resolved to a broken version of `jekyll-picture-tag`
questions.explanation: `jekyll-picture-tag` is not on the safelist published at pages.github.com/versions. The remote `--safe` build silently ignores it, so the Liquid tags resolve to nothing and you get plain `` output.

questions.question: You can either keep the GitHub Pages built-in build (with `github-pages` in your Gemfile) or move to a GitHub Actions workflow. Which is the strongest argument for the Actions route, given a typical engineering blog?
choices: Actions is the only way to deploy to a custom domain
The built-in build is being deprecated next quarter
Actions lets you pick any Jekyll version and any gems, including non-safelisted plugins and your own `_plugins/` scripts
Actions produces smaller `_site/` output than the built-in build
questions.explanation: The built-in build pins Jekyll and the plugin set via the `github-pages` meta-gem. Actions runs your `Gemfile` as written, so any gem or custom plugin works. Custom domains and `_site/` size are unaffected by which build path you choose.
